decision: After the builder agent terminated mid-stream (implementation written, tests + verify missing), re-spawned the same builder role to finish the job — write the five tests and run verify — explicitly instructing it to leave the existing implementation as-is.
alternative_not_taken: Writing the tests and running verify myself in the orchestrator context, or re-spawning the builder to rebuild from scratch.
rationale: Writing the tests myself would break the role boundary the orchestrator must hold (it composes roles, never does their work). A from-scratch rebuild would discard a sound, already-inspected implementation. Finishing via the builder keeps the work inside the build role and is the least wasteful path.
